heading: Conditional Reading
body: **Conditional method** translates an "unless" sentence as an if-then statement. Instead of replacing "unless" with $\vee$, this method builds a conditional structure.

examples.text: "The game will be played unless it rains" can be read as an if-then statement.

note: This lesson uses the conditional method only.

heading: Negate The Exception
body: In this method, the clause after "unless" is denied first. That negated clause becomes the left side of the conditional.

examples.text: In "The game will be played unless it rains," the clause after "unless" is "it rains."

heading: Translation Pattern
body: Translate "$P$ unless $Q$" as $\sim Q \rightarrow P$. Negate the statement after "unless," then use $\rightarrow$, then write the other statement.

examples.text: "The game will be played unless it rains" becomes $\sim R \rightarrow G$.
"The game will be played unless it rains" becomes $R \rightarrow G$.

heading: Simple Cases
body: Choose a letter for each statement. Then negate the unless-part and place the other statement after $\rightarrow$.

examples.text: Let $L$ = The lamp is on, $P$ = The power fails. "The lamp is on unless the power fails" becomes $\sim P \rightarrow L$.
Let $B$ = The bus arrives, $S$ = The street is blocked. "The bus arrives unless the street is blocked" becomes $\sim S \rightarrow B$.
Let $D$ = The door opens, $K$ = The key is lost. "The door opens unless the key is lost" becomes $\sim D \rightarrow K$.

heading: Do Not Reverse
body: A common mistake is putting the main statement on the left and the negated unless-part on the right. The negated unless-part must be the antecedent.

examples.text: "The store opens unless the road is closed" becomes $\sim R \rightarrow S$.
"The store opens unless the road is closed" becomes $S \rightarrow \sim R$.

note: Read the pattern as "if not $Q$, then $P$."

heading: Core Pattern
body: Translate "$P$ unless $Q$" by the conditional method as $\sim Q \rightarrow P$. Negate the unless-part, then write the other statement as the consequent.
